courseTitle: Blocks & Attributes — Efficient Reuse and Titleblock Data
courseDescription: Practical guide for creating and managing blocks, attributes, dynamic blocks, and titleblock metadata for municipal drawings. Focus on consistency, attribute extraction, and avoiding common beginner pitfalls.
courseObjectives: Create reusable blocks for common symbols and fixtures
Define and extract block attributes for titleblocks and schedules
Use dynamic blocks for parametric doors/windows
Manage block libraries and avoid broken references during submission

phases.phaseDescription: Understand the advantages of blocks for consistency and file size reduction.

steps.title: What is a Block?
steps.subtitle: Blocks are grouped objects saved under a name
steps.why: Blocks let you reuse symbols (doors, windows, trees) and update all instances by editing the definition.
instructions: Select objects to include and type BLOCK to create an in-file block definition.
Use WBLOCK to write a block to a separate .dwg file for a shared library.
Insert blocks using INSERT or the DesignCenter for fast reuse.

note.label: Info
note.text: Use descriptive names (DOOR_SWING_900, WINDOW_1200x900) to keep libraries searchable.

phases.phaseDescription: How to build clean, annotative-ready blocks that play well with layers and styles.

steps.title: Define Base Point and Layers
steps.subtitle: Choose a logical insertion point and set ByLayer properties
steps.why: Correct base point ensures easy insertion; ByLayer keeps appearance consistent.
instructions: When creating BLOCK, set the base point at a logical snap (e.g., door hinge or center).
Set block contents to use ByLayer for color/linetype so inserted instances inherit the target layer properties.
Avoid embedding text with explicit properties; use ByLayer text or attributes instead.

steps.title: Use WBLOCK for Library Files
steps.subtitle: Keep a shared blocks folder for project reuse
steps.why: External block libraries allow team-wide consistency and reduce duplication.
instructions: Select the block or objects and type WBLOCK to export to a .dwg in your BLOCKS library folder.
Reference these via DesignCenter or insert them when needed. Avoid XREF'ing blocks unless managed centrally.

phases.phaseDescription: Add dynamic text fields to blocks for metadata and create extractable schedules.

steps.title: Create Block Attributes (ATTDEF)
steps.subtitle: Add attributes for project name, scale, date
steps.why: Attributes let you store structured data (project, owner) inside blocks for automatic extraction.
instructions: Type ATTDEF to create an attribute: set tag (PROJECT_NAME), prompt, default value, and text style (use TXT_MUNI).
Place attributes in your TITLEBLOCK block so each insertion can be filled with project-specific data.
Use BATTMAN to manage attribute visibility and order.

steps.title: Extract Attributes to a Schedule
steps.subtitle: Use EATTEXT or ATTEXT for export
steps.why: Municipal forms often need a table of project data; attributes let you export consistent values easily.
instructions: Use DATAEXTRACTION wizard to create a table from block attributes and insert it into the drawing or export to CSV.
Alternatively use ATTEXTRACT tools or scripts to pull attributes for external reports.

phases.phaseDescription: Create flexible blocks (doors/windows) that change size without multiple definitions.

steps.title: Basic Dynamic Block Parameters
steps.subtitle: Visibility, Stretch, Flip parameters
steps.why: Dynamic blocks reduce the number of block types and speed drafting.
instructions: Open BEDIT to edit block definition and add parameters (e.g., Linear parameter for door width).
Add actions (Stretch, Array) and test with the Test Block tool inside BEDIT.
Save and reload block; update instances as needed.

note.label: Info
note.text: Start simple: implement size parameter for doors before adding complex visibility states.

phases.phaseDescription: Best practices for using blocks in multi-sheet projects and avoiding broken references.

steps.title: Insert and Replace Blocks Safely
steps.subtitle: Use Insert or DesignCenter
steps.why: Consistent insertion avoids mismatched scales and broken links.
instructions: Insert blocks at 1:1 and let the target layer control appearance. If the block is external, ensure the .dwg path is relative within project folder.
To update a block across drawings, replace definition in a master file and use INSERT with same name or use BlockReplace plugin.

steps.title: Avoid Exploding Blocks Unnecessarily
steps.subtitle: Exploding breaks attribute links and consistency
steps.why: Exploding destroys the block’s future editability and can break attribute data.
instructions: Only explode when you absolutely must (for one-off edits).
If you need to edit a block instance, use REFEDIT or BEDIT to edit the definition instead.

phases.phaseDescription: Keep libraries clean and ensure blocks will survive municipal review and team sharing.

steps.title: Purge and Audit Block Library
steps.subtitle: Use PURGE and AUDIT to clean files
steps.why: Unused or corrupted blocks increase file size and cause errors during submission.
instructions: Run PURGE to remove unused block definitions and styles. Run AUDIT to fix file issues.
Export key blocks to WBLOCK into a library folder for reuse across projects.
Version your library and avoid changing names of widely used blocks unexpectedly.

steps.title: Titleblock Attribute Check Before Submission
steps.subtitle: Verify all attributes are filled
steps.why: Municipality reviewers expect completed metadata such as project name, owner, and drafter.
instructions: Use BATTMAN to check attribute order and visibility in the titleblock.
Run DATAEXTRACTION to produce a CSV of attributes and confirm values are correct before final PDF export.
Lock the titleblock layer in layouts to prevent accidental edits.
